# Snip

> A visual whiteboard between you and your AI agent.

Snip is an open-source menu bar app (macOS + Linux) that lets AI agents render visual output and get human feedback with annotations. Agents render diagrams, HTML, posters, or UI components through Snip's CLI; the human approves or annotates directly on the output; the agent gets structured feedback and iterates.

## Key Capabilities

- Render Mermaid diagrams and show to user for review
- Render arbitrary HTML (components, email templates, posters, layouts) and show to user for review
- Open any image for annotated review with approve/reject workflow
- Search screenshot library by natural language description
- Extract text from images via OCR
- Standalone screenshot capture with annotation tools (arrows, rectangles, text, tags, blur, AI segmentation)
- Local AI-powered screenshot organization via Ollama (naming, tagging, categorization, semantic search)

## CLI Usage

Snip's CLI is the primary interface for AI agents. It works with any agent that can run shell commands (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Codex).

```
# Render a Mermaid diagram for user review
echo 'graph TD; A-->B-->C' | snip render --format mermaid --message "Does this look right?"

# Render HTML for user review
echo '<h1>Hello</h1><p>Preview</p>' | snip render --format html --message "Review this layout"

# Open an image for annotated review
snip open screenshot.png --message "Is the layout correct?"

# Search screenshots
snip search "login page error"

# Extract text from an image
snip transcribe screenshot.png
```

## Review Flow

When `snip render` or `snip open` is called, Snip opens a visual editor where the user can:
- Approve the output
- Annotate with arrows, rectangles, text, tags, blur
- Type text feedback
- Request changes

The CLI returns structured JSON:
```json
{
  "status": "approved" | "changes_requested",
  "edited": true,
  "path": "/path/to/annotated-image.png",
  "text": "Optional text feedback from user"
}
```

## MCP Server

For agents without shell access, Snip also provides an MCP server (stdio transport) with tools: render_diagram, open_in_snip, search_screenshots, list_screenshots, get_screenshot, transcribe_screenshot, organize_screenshot, get_categories, install_extension.

## Install

macOS: brew install --cask rixinhahaha/snip/snip
Linux: Download AppImage or deb from GitHub releases

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/rixinhahaha/snip
- Website: https://snipit.dev
- License: MIT
